title: Heteranthera rotundifolia
intro: Heteranthera rotundifolia, commonly known as round-leaf mudflat lily or cabomba, is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Heteranthera rotundifolia include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, teas, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Heteranthera rotundifolia is classified within the family Alismataceae. This plant belongs to the order Alismatales. It is a species of flowering plant that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. Heteranthera rotundifolia is a member of the genus Heteranthera, which is a group of aquatic plants. The classification of Heteranthera rotundifolia reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its aquatic environment.
uses: Heteranthera rotundifolia, also known as the round-leaf heteranthera, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
